name: Torre Baro via the Besos River Park - circular route in Barcelona
name_original: Torre Baró por el parque fluvial del Besòs - circular por Barcelona
region_intro: Touring cycling around Barcelones offers a diverse range of terrain, from urban routes to coastal paths and hilly natural parks. The region is characterized by its Mediterranean coastline, the Llobregat and Besòs river deltas, and the Collserola mountain range, providing varied elevation and scenic viewpoints. Extensive bike lanes and natural features bike routes Barcelona area make it accessible for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Barcelones The most popular touring cycling route is Plaza del Mar – Beach Bike Lane loop from Urgell, a 9.8 miles (15.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 1 minute to complete. This easy route follows the city's coastline, offering flat terrain and sea views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is City tour through Barcelona - from Arco de Triunfo to the mouth of the Besòs, an easy 13.5 miles (21.7 km) path. This route explores urban landmarks and follows the Besòs River Park. Local touring cyclists also love the Plaza del Mar – Magic Fountain of Montjuïc loop from Ciutadella | Vila Olímpica, a 10.5 miles (17.0 km) trail leading through urban areas and up Montjuïc hill, often completed in about 1 hour 9 minutes. Touring cycling around Barcelones is defined by coastal paths, urban green spaces, and the foothills of the Collserola mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy city rides to more challenging climbs. The routes in Barcelones are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1,100 reviews. More than 46,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Barcelones's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Barcelones?
A: Barcelones offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 275 options available on komoot. These routes cater to various preferences, from easy coastal rides to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes for all skill levels in Barcelones?
A: Yes, Barcelones provides touring cycling routes suitable for all skill levels. You'll find 89 easy routes, 68 moderate routes, and 118 difficult routes, ensuring options for beginners, intermediate cyclists, and experienced riders seeking a challenge. The terrain varies from flat urban and coastal paths to hilly natural parks like Collserola.

Q: What natural features characterize the touring cycling routes around Barcelones?
A: The touring cycling routes in Barcelones are defined by a diverse landscape. You can expect to cycle along the Mediterranean coastline, explore the Llobregat and Besòs river deltas, and ascend into the foothills of the Collserola mountain range. These areas offer a mix of lush forests, rugged mountains, and scenic viewpoints overlooking the city and sea.

Q: What landmarks or viewpoints can I explore on touring cycling routes in Barcelones?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Barcelones pass by significant landmarks and offer stunning viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38233654" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza del Mar – Magic Fountain of Montjuïc loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3976347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magic Fountain of Montjuïc&lt;/a&gt;. Other routes might lead you to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7478049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monumental Cascade in Parc de la Ciutadella&lt;/a&gt;, or offer panoramic views from spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1438194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cypresses Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3124558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miramar Gardens&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Barcelones?
A: Absolutely. Barcelones offers numerous family-friendly touring cycling routes, particularly along its flat coastal paths and within urban green spaces. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32272884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza del Mar – Beach Bike Lane loop from Urgell&lt;/a&gt; are easy and provide safe, scenic rides suitable for families. The city's commitment to cycling infrastructure, including dedicated bike lanes, makes it a comfortable environment for cyclists of all ages.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Barcelones?
A: Yes, circular touring cycling routes are plentiful in Barcelones. Many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e973328299" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre Baro via the Besos River Park - circular route in Barcelona&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate challenge while exploring urban areas and natural parks.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Barcelones?
A: The favorable climate of Barcelona makes touring cycling enjoyable almost year-round. However, the best times are typically outside of the peak summer months (July and August) when temperatures can be very high. Spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) offer pleasant temperatures and less crowded routes, ideal for exploring the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there cafes or amenities along the touring cycling routes in Barcelones?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Barcelones, especially those within the city or along popular coastal areas, have easy access to cafes, restaurants, and other amenities. Urban routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970627967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City tour through Barcelona - from Arco de Triunfo to the mouth of the Besòs&lt;/a&gt; will keep you close to urban conveniences. Even routes venturing into natural parks often have facilities at their entry points or in nearby towns.

Q: How accessible are touring cycling routes in Barcelones by public transport?
A: Barcelones boasts excellent public transport access, making it easy to reach many touring cycling routes. The city has an expanding network of bike lanes and public transport options that can accommodate bicycles, particularly outside of peak hours. This allows cyclists to easily connect to routes starting further afield or to return from point-to-point journeys.

Q: What are some of the more challenging touring cycling routes in Barcelones?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Barcelones offers several difficult touring cycling routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32665812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collserola Tower – Turó d'en Corts viewpoint loop from Fontana&lt;/a&gt;, for example, features over 580 meters of ascent. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38213427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collserola Tower – Cerdanyola - Forat del Vent loop from Sarrià&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 750 meters of climbing through the Collserola Natural Park.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Barcelones?
A: The touring cycling routes in Barcelones are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1,100 reviews. Reviewers often praise the region's diverse terrain, from the stunning coastal views to the challenging climbs in the Collserola mountains, as well as the well-maintained paths and accessibility to urban amenities.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that explore Barcelona's coastline?
A: Yes, Barcelona's coastline offers fantastic touring cycling opportunities. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32272884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza del Mar – Beach Bike Lane loop from Urgell&lt;/a&gt; provide leisurely, flat rides with stunning sea views, connecting various beaches from Barceloneta to Nova Icària and Mar Bella. The seafront section of the Ronda Verda also offers miles of coastal cycling.

Q: Can I combine touring cycling with exploring Barcelona's urban attractions?
A: Absolutely. Barcelona's extensive network of bike lanes makes urban cycling an enjoyable way to explore the city's iconic landmarks. Routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970627967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City tour through Barcelona - from Arco de Triunfo to the mouth of the Besòs&lt;/a&gt; allow you to cycle past attractions like the Arc de Triomf and explore the Besòs River Park, blending cultural sightseeing with your ride.
tips: Park with river access. There is a very well marked and wide bike path.

From here you have a great panoramic view of Nou Barris, a district in the northeast of Barcelona. On the other hand, the old Torre del Baró castle is a real eye catcher. So the view is great all around.
Spectacular views of Barcelona from this crossroads called Forat del Vent.
